title: helianthemum apenninum var. tomentellum
intro: Helianthemum apenninum var. tomentellum, also known as the Alpine Rockrose, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties and provide several health benefits, including antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Helianthemum apenninum var. tomentellum can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve for topical application, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
